title: Xiao Tuanzi's Secret Freshman Survival Guide Revealed!

sections.content: School is about to start — have all you incoming CSUers been scrolling through countless "freshman orientation guides" online? From dorm essentials to newbie tips, and even how to become a top achiever starting from your very first year.
But the quality of online "guides" varies wildly, so Xiao Tuanzi has put together a set of tips that college students truly need to know — the Freshman Guide (CSU Edition).
Strong Legs

Having "strong legs" is the foundation of a happy life at CSU. With them, you can breeze through the CSU campus run, sprint before deadlines, skillfully hit the "brakes" on the busy Qingshui Road during rush hour to stay safe, and dodge the chaotic foot traffic at Cafeteria No. 2 during meal times to secure your spot in line.
Skilled Riding

During your time at CSU, commuting might drive you crazy. After all, Central South University is a truly "massive" school with six campuses. Master this skill and you can teleport from your dorm to your 8 AM class in 10 minutes, saving precious sleep. Achieve "dining freedom" — with so many restaurants around the university town, you can eat wherever you want.
Sharp Eyes

Your college life will be inseparable from the word "find." Finding your food delivery among the piles stacked outside the dorm, locating your e-bike in the spectacular sea of electric scooters... But if you have a pair of "sharp eyes," none of this will be a problem.
Quick Fingers

To further improve your quality of life at CSU, you'll need lightning-fast fingers. Accidentally play audio out loud in the library? Quick fingers can save you from embarrassment. During CSU's fierce course selection battles, fast fingers help you grab your preferred classes. Want to go home for the National Day holiday? Let your quick fingers help you snag those tickets.
A Strong Heart

Xiao Tuanzi also recommends equipping yourself with a "strong heart," because a resilient heart determines how happy you'll be. When you see someone unplug an e-bike charger before it's fully charged, speak up boldly. When someone cuts in line at Yuxiu Building, step up bravely and say, "Please wait your turn."
Congratulations — you've mastered the secret to a happy college life: speak up when you see a problem, let others deal with the stress, and keep the joy for yourself!

Freshman guides are everywhere online, and the pressure to "win at the starting line" never stops. But college isn't just a platform for learning and broadening your horizons — it's also a stage for discovering and improving yourself. Xiao Tuanzi hopes you'll stay driven without burning out, and enjoy this journey with clarity and determination!

title: Ahem! A Proper Guide for New Students~

sections.content: Welcome to the Central South University family!

By the Xiang River, at the foot of Yuelu Mountain, we look forward to embarking on a new chapter full of challenges and opportunities with you. To help all new students smoothly transition from high schoolers to college students, we've specially prepared the "CSU Experience Handbook." Everything you want to know about Central South University — Xiao Tuanzi has the answers.
Changsha: The Dazzling Star City

Changsha, also known as "Star City," is the capital of Hunan Province and serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of the province. This city, where history and modernity intertwine, unfolds like a flowing scroll, waiting for you to explore.

The maple leaves of Yuelu Mountain and the waters of the Xiang River complement each other beautifully, accompanying generations of diligent students. The statue of Chairman Mao standing on Orange Isle bears witness to the city's patriotic spirit. The shimmering waves of Meixi Lake and the bustling Wuyi Square weave together Changsha's tranquility and vitality. Here, every step is history, every glance is scenery — Changsha tells the thousand-year story of this famous Chu-Han city in its own unique way.

Of course, Changsha also captivates visitors with its unique food culture. Here, every dish is an ultimate temptation for your taste buds. A bowl of freshly fried stinky tofu — crispy outside and tender inside, bursting with aroma — paired with a signature Cha Yan drink for a refreshing balance. Add in various sizzling Hunan dishes, fragrant and spicy with endless flavors. The taste of Changsha is all in that one satisfying bite.
Over the four or more years ahead, you'll have plenty of free time to experience Changsha's unique cultural charm and way of life, and to feel the warmth and beauty of this city firsthand.

But do be aware — Star City Changsha has unpredictable weather: damp spring rains, scorching summer heat, fierce autumn winds, and biting winter cold. Make sure to dress appropriately to avoid catching a cold!
Central South University: Majestic Yuelu, Mighty Xiang River

Towering ancient trees, lush greenery, natural landscapes and cultural sites complementing each other, traditional culture and modern civilization in harmonious coexistence. Every landmark on CSU's campus tells a different story and carries unique memories. Let's explore the campus from a first-person perspective and visit those unforgettable scenic spots!

Green ripples sway, evening breeze carries fragrance. The lotus pond at the South Campus is a tranquil retreat. Here you'll find green leaves and red flowers, as well as snow-capped pavilion roofs. Lean against the railing to admire the lotus, breathe in the delicate fragrance — a special experience; pause occasionally and feel the harmony between knowledge and nature.

Moving on, we arrive at the main gate of the Main Campus. Built in 1954, the inscription on the gate has changed twice — from the original "Central South Institute of Mining and Metallurgy" to today's "Central South University." With a steady presence, it welcomes every dream-filled freshman and sees off every graduating student.

Walking through the gate, you're immediately struck by a sense of history. Winding paths, gray tiles and red bricks — the Democracy Building and Peace Building, two historic structures, transport you back to a scholarly era with their classical elegance.

Past the Main Campus, following the sparkling Jade Belt River, we reach the New Campus. The Bird's Nest Gymnasium here, with its modern design and grand scale, has become a new campus landmark. Inside, the energy is contagious — you can work up a sweat and showcase your youthful vitality.
A bit further ahead are the ABCD Teaching Buildings, each connected by sky bridges. On a nice day, the soft sunset glow and the students on the bridge together make a beautiful scene.

When we turn our gaze to the Xiangya Campus, the striking Red Building comes into view. With its upturned eaves and red-brick structure, it embodies the spirit of healing and dedication to medicine, teaching, and saving lives.

Finally, let's end this journey at the Railway Campus's "Train Museum." Connecting history at one end and the future at the other, the stationary locomotives are like thick history books, telling the glorious story of China's railway heritage.

As the seasons change, these landmarks constantly shift their colors and atmosphere. Spring's revival brings brightness and warmth, letting you feel CSU's unique hope and vitality amid blooming flowers and lush greenery. Summer evenings by the Back Lake, rushing to the sports field for a CSU rock music festival while there's still time. Autumn's presence quietly arrives with red maple leaves falling along the paths. Winter's first snow blankets the earth in white, bringing students pure joy.

Every landmark on CSU's campus is a story, and every building carries a piece of history. Together they paint the rich and colorful cultural tapestry of Central South University. CSU's beauty goes beyond words — its vivid charm awaits your personal exploration.

title: Club Fair! Let's Go Go Go~

sections.content: Maple flames ignite dreams, a hundred clubs converge. The Central South University Club Fair is about to begin! Over a hundred clubs gather to showcase their unique styles. Whether you're into science and innovation, arts, social practice, or sports, you'll find like-minded companions here and let your youth shine through your passions!
Not sure what clubs CSU has? Don't worry — the full list of over a hundred clubs is ready for you. Follow Xiao Nan's footsteps and unlock the wonderful world of CSU clubs~
Academic Clubs (26)
Arts Clubs (22)
Technology Clubs (11)
Sports Clubs (26)
Charity Clubs (12)
Practice Clubs (13)

Clubs are buzzing with energy, and youth is ready to connect. In this dazzling world of clubs, there's always a place that can become your "sanctuary." The enrollment channel is ready: during the Club Fair, just scan the QR code at the booth to join the group chat and hop on board!
Friendly reminder: Clubs are great, but manage your energy wisely. Pick based on your interests — don't let enthusiasm be worn down by overcommitment!

title: Eating Everything Only Makes Me Nutritionally Balanced 🤤

sections.content: Want to stay healthy? It takes good food as well as exercise! Want regional specialties? Chinese and Western cuisines are all here! Need an energy boost? Breakfast through late-night snacks have you covered! Want to try something new every day? We guarantee your appetite will be satisfied!

At CSU, you'll encounter thousands of delicious foods. What are the campus dishes with rave reviews? What do CSUers eat every day? Xiao Tuanzi is here to take you on a food tour of CSU — let's dive into this ocean of culinary delights.
South Campus / Cafeteria No. 2
Located near the South Campus entrance, Cafeteria No. 2 can not only satisfy your stomach but also perfectly cover all three meals of the day.

The first floor features a dazzling array of specialty snacks — pastries, stir-fries, fried rice, noodles, malatang, wontons, and more. Absolutely irresistible. Plus tempting desserts, with sweet aromas filling the air.

The second floor Chinese restaurant serves all kinds of home-style dishes with a cozy, homey feel. Sip a spoonful of hot soup and savor its rich warmth; slurp a mouthful of noodles and feel the taste of home dancing on your tongue. That's the taste of everyday life, and the taste of happiness.

The third floor specializes in regional dishes from across China — all eight major cuisines represented. Small plates of Liuyang steamed dishes, Chongqing chicken hotpot — a full bowl of pure satisfaction.

The fourth floor has a halal restaurant, a Chinese-Western restaurant, and a self-service buffet. Lamb pao mo, Lanzhou hand-pulled noodles, big plate chicken — rich variety with no repeats, guaranteed to fill you up. The self-service restaurant awaits, pick as much as you like.
South Campus / Cafeteria No. 8
An elegant dining environment with exquisite dishes, big-screen TVs to accompany your meal — this internet-famous cafeteria lives up to its reputation.
The first floor offers stir-fries, stews, fried foods, desserts, soups, fruits, and drinks — all in one place. Generous portions, diverse menu, hard not to fall for every dish.
The second floor's self-service restaurant is simply amazing — drinks, fruit platters, cold dishes, hot dishes. Sweet, sour, bitter, spicy, all in your bowl.
Feel like a group outing? The third floor has hotpot and mala stir-fry. A big steaming pot — who wouldn't love an on-campus team dinner?
Main Campus / Cafeteria No. 35

Cafeteria No. 35's dumplings bring comfort from the north, giving students far from home a taste of family. Thin skin, generous filling, many varieties, affordable prices. Beyond the signature dumplings, the self-service section is absolutely worth raving about — just looking at the colorful dishes makes your mouth water. The big pot soups are hearty and flavorful.

Upstairs is the halal restaurant — big plate chicken, naan-wrapped meat, beef hand-pulled noodles, everything you could want. Icy drinks in summer, steaming hot soups in winter — a companion through all four seasons.
Railway Campus / Cafeteria No. 4 & Flavor Cafeteria

The first floor is a food court, the second floor is a halal cafeteria. Besides the buffet, they serve roast duck rice, big plate chicken, Italian pasta at lunch, cakes in the evening, stir-fried noodles, braised chicken — all making an appearance, easily satisfying the dining needs of Railway Campus students and staff. After your last class, enjoy a Railway Campus dessert and happily bid today's fatigue farewell.
The impeccable authentic Hunan flavors will definitely capture your stomach.
Xiangya Cafeteria
The first floor meets dining needs at every hour, from breakfast to late-night snacks. Regularly updated menus always bring pleasant surprises.

The second floor is a self-service restaurant with moderate portions, rich variety, and free choice. Plus regularly updated braised pot specials. Even in Changsha, you can taste cuisine from all over the country.
The halal restaurant offers a diverse, ever-changing menu. Handmade noodles topped with secret-recipe beef slices — a bowl of steaming hot noodles that always warms the heart.
Back Lake Area

Shenghua Cold Dishes: A classic CSU cold dish spot, a taste of student memories. A Yuan Snail Noodles: Changsha's best snail noodles, delicious whether dry-mixed or stir-fried. Xianmiao Roast Chicken: Street food vibes, with glistening roast chicken that's unforgettable in one bite. Countless shops await your discovery.
Lushan South Road

Stinky Tofu: Crispy outside, tender inside, fried to perfection — worth the wait. Tianma Beef Pancakes: Generous filling, crispy crust — a trusted classic. Xianfeng Bakery: Whole wheat or sweet, there's always one that's your favorite. Lushan South Road truly deserves to be called CSU students' second cafeteria.

Think that's all? Not even close. Besides main meals, Shenghua Plaza also has Luckin Coffee, Mixue Ice City, and other drink shops — come grab your "first milk tea of the semester"! On top of that, there are all kinds of snack and dessert shops both on and off campus. Foodies, rejoice!

Did you get this healthy and practical CSU food guide? At CSU, we don't just aim for the stars — we also stroll through life and savor every aroma. Grab your chopsticks and be the first to try! Which CSU dish will be your favorite "exclusive memory"?

title: Xiao Tuanzi Reminder: Grab These Commuting Tips Now

sections.content: At the sprawling CSU campus, e-bikes have always been the primary commuting tool for CSUers. No matter which part of campus you need to reach, you can hop on your beloved e-bike and go. Though the distance is long, riding into a pleasant breeze can chase away the blues. But commuting always comes with its share of headaches. Xiao Tuanzi has selected some different routes — let's look at the various troubles CSUers face.
01: Not Slowing Down at Speed Bumps Leads to Painful Tumbles
Trouble spot: Intersection of Shenghua Buildings 5 and 6

sections.alt: Speed bumps at Shenghua Buildings 5 and 6

sections.content: Speed bumps on campus serve as both a visible reminder and invisible protection for CSUers. But they can't stop those "students fast as the wind" from being "unfazed" at the sight of them. The result? A spectacular wipeout.
Xiao Tuanzi's tip: When you encounter a speed bump ahead, be patient and slow down. Never try to fly over speed bumps!
02: Even Eagle Eyes Can't Escape Blind Spots
Trouble spot: Near Cafeteria No. 8

Even if you can see in all directions and hear everything, campus blind spots always catch you off guard — a nightmare for cyclists. At corners, between buildings — one second a sudden encounter, the next a heart-pounding near-miss.
Xiao Tuanzi's tip: Control your speed and don't be a "speed racer." At corners and blind spots, always carefully check for oncoming vehicles before proceeding safely.
03: Going Downhill in Style, Crashing in Shame
Trouble spot: Dormitory Buildings South 7 and South 8

Downhill slopes are often a pain point for CSUers. Speed and thrill, inertia and gravity, lane changes and sliding — downhills have a thousand temptations. If you're not careful, confidence and riding skills can only result in a crash.
Xiao Tuanzi's tip: Downhill is not an accelerator — always be ready to brake!
04: Eye Contact on a One-Way Street Won't Spark Romance, Just Traffic Jams
Trouble spot: Shenghua Building 19

There's a kind of awkwardness where two people stare at each other but can't move forward. A kind of helplessness where neither side can win, and someone must yield. A kind of direction that isn't where your heart wants to go, yet you collide head-on. One-way street gridlock is every student's frustration.
Xiao Tuanzi's tip: Don't take chances and ride the wrong way on a one-way street!
05: Hogging the Road Isn't Winning
Trouble spot: CSU Student Apartment Bus Stop
Escaping the crowd doesn't mean riding recklessly in the motor vehicle lane. Joining the crowd doesn't mean crashing into people about to board the bus.
Xiao Tuanzi's tip: E-bikes should ride in the non-motorized lane and must not block the bus stop area!

Xiao Tuanzi advises all CSUers: reckless riding means tears for loved ones. Dear CSU students, set aside the rebelliousness and be a rule-abiding cyclist. Follow traffic rules, yield courteously, ride carefully — be a safe-riding CSUer, and you can truly say goodbye to commuting troubles.

title: Save This! Score Check for Returning Students, Exam Guide for Freshmen (CET-4/6 Edition)

sections.content: The National Education Examinations Authority has announced that the score inquiry service for the first half of 2025's College English Test (CET) Band 4 and Band 6 will open on August 16, 2025, at 6:00 AM.
Opening Time
August 16, 2025, 6:00 AM
What You Can Check
First half of 2025 College English Test (CET) Band 4 and Band 6 scores
How to Check
1. National Education Examinations website: http://cet.neea.edu.cn/cet
2. NEEA WeChat Mini Program: Open the WeChat app and search for the "National Education Examinations" mini program.
3. NEEA Alipay Mini Program: Open the Alipay app and scan the QR code or search for the "National Education Examinations" mini program.
4. NEEA Baidu Mini Program: Open the Baidu app and scan the QR code or search for the "National Education Examinations" mini program.
Electronic Score Report
Starting from 3:00 PM on August 17, students can log in to the NEEA website (www.neea.edu.cn) to download their electronic score report for free.

That covers the score inquiry details for returning students. Now let's turn to the freshmen about to enter CSU. You might still have lots of questions about CET-4/6 — don't worry, Xiao Tuanzi is here as your "Q&A assistant" to answer all your CET questions~
Frequently Asked Questions

1. What is CET-4/6 and why does it matter? The College English Test (CET) is a national standardized exam organized by the Ministry of Education to assess English proficiency among college students. CET exams are held twice a year, in June and December. It's an important measure of college students' English ability and has a significant impact on your future development~
2. What score counts as passing? Scores of 220 and above receive an official score report. There is no official passing line, but a CET-4 score of 425 or above is required to register for CET-6.

3. Are there grade restrictions for registration? Freshmen can take CET-4 right away. Xiao Tuanzi's secret tip: take advantage of your high school knowledge while it's still fresh and pass CET-4 as soon as possible~ If you've taken CET-6 and scored 425 or above but want to retake it, you must skip one exam session — you won't be able to register for the next CET-6 and will have to wait until the following year. Plan your exam schedule ahead, since CET exams are only held every six months~

4. Is there a limit on how many times I can take the exam? At our university, once you pass CET-4, you cannot retake it. If you miss an exam or violate rules, you'll be banned for one session. For example, if you oversleep and miss the December CET-4, you won't be able to register for the following June's CET-4. Xiao Tuanzi's advice: unless there are unavoidable circumstances, always show up for exams you've registered for~

5. Why is my CET score 0? A written test score of 0 can occur in these situations: (1) Absence or rule violation results in a 0 score; (2) No absence or violation, but scoring below 220 results in a 0; (3) No absence or violation, but failing to attach the barcode during the exam results in a 0. As a major national exam, be sure to follow all exam procedures properly~

6. Should I register for the speaking test? It depends on your personal needs. If you need it for specific purposes or certifications, go ahead and register. But it's not mandatory — the speaking test score is not included in your total score.
Finally, we wish everyone satisfying CET scores. May all returning and new students pass every exam they take!
